title: Wekan vs Trello for Solo users
meta_description: Wekan vs Trello for solo users who want visual kanban boards without running servers, updates, or self-hosted infrastructure.
constraint_lens: You want a visual kanban board that works without maintaining servers, updates, or technical infrastructure.

verdict.summary: Trello wins for solo users who want a visual kanban board without running infrastructure. It provides hosted boards that work immediately after creating an account. Wekan requires installing and maintaining a self-hosted instance with server updates. If self-hosting and server updates must be maintained to keep the board running, Wekan fails first.
verdict.decision_rule: If self-hosting and server updates must be maintained to keep the board running, Wekan fails first.

sections.heading: Best fit for maintenance-free visual task boards
sections.content: You maintain personal kanban boards and refuse to manage integrations or infrastructure. Trello provides hosted boards that run immediately in the browser. Wekan typically requires installing and maintaining a server environment where the board is hosted.

bullets.why_it_matters: You run the board on your own server and control where task data is stored.
You can modify the software or customize deployments if you manage infrastructure.

bullets.point: Local deployment possible inside private environments
bullets.why_it_matters: Boards can run entirely within internal networks rather than relying on external services.

bullets.point: Hosted kanban boards accessible from the browser
bullets.why_it_matters: You start using boards immediately without installing server software.
You update task stages visually without configuring infrastructure.

bullets.point: Automatic platform updates handled by the service
bullets.why_it_matters: The board keeps working without server upgrades or maintenance tasks.

sections.heading: Where each tool can break down

items.fails_when: You must maintain server updates or troubleshoot hosting issues to keep boards running.
items.what_to_do_instead: Use Trello for a board that works without infrastructure maintenance.

items.fails_when: You require full control over hosting and cannot depend on cloud services.
items.what_to_do_instead: Switch to Wekan if self-hosting and local control are essential.

sections.heading: When this verdict might flip
sections.content: If you already maintain a personal server and want full control over board hosting and data storage, Wekan may become practical.

sections.heading: Quick rules
rules: If you want a kanban board with no server maintenance, choose Trello.
If hosting and updates sound like ongoing work, avoid Wekan.
If full control over hosting matters more than convenience, consider Wekan.

faqs.a: Yes. It is typically installed on a server environment that you maintain.
Yes. Boards run on Trello servers and do not require installation.
Trello requires less maintenance because hosting and updates are handled automatically.

faqs.q: When would Wekan be the better choice?
faqs.a: It can be better if you need full control over hosting and prefer running the board locally.
